feedback.text: In concern to the Digital Service act, I would like to see another, sixth point added to it: Not allowing companies to delete or remove consumers access to video games which the consumers have already bought. If companies are allowed to do it, it could set a precedent where companies will make the expiration dates of games closer and closer. Any consumer could tell you that when they bought the game, they didnt consciously sign up to it being deleted without warning in the future. I believe this is a very important part of consumers rights, and can and is already having global consequences on the gaming industry. Doing nothing now will only embolden the companies to delete more games. I myself have not had a game of mine deleted, but Im afraid it is inevitable if this keeps going.
